Curious what’s holding people back at this stage.

item.title: Why do workers/poor hate investing? — 67 replies — 268.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why do workers/poor hate investing?

Open thread • Replies: 67 • Updated: 2026-05-31 18:43 UTC

OP

Most conversation with friends/family/coworkers I have when it comes to investing long term in Roth IRA, high yield savings, and my ETF Dividend portfolio even with all the money I make working cyber security utilizing FIRE method they get so aggressive about “what if” scenarios thinking I’m day-trading and even when I explain it they don’t get it.

“WHAT IF THE MARKET ALL GOES UNDER AND YOU LOSE EVERYTHING”

“WHAT IF YOU TAKE ALL YOUR MONEY OUT AND SELL ON A LOSS”

“WHAT IF SOMETHING BAD HAPPENS AND YOU HAVE NO MONEY”

Which I retort what happens when you lose your job? When the market goes under and suddenly you’re up for layoffs?

“WELL THAT WILL NEVER HAPPEN BECAUSE IM VALUABLE AND MY SKILLS AND MY TIME AR COMPANY AND-“

Like do these people really think working for someone for the rest of your life is the only way to live? I’d rather live frugally but free, and that really seems to irk people.


What the hell is this psychology? Why do they hate the idea someone doesn’t wanna work for someone for the rest of their life or majority? Do we forget money is to buy our freedom?

Early replies (first 3)

62294872
They are physically, mentally incapable of visualizing what you are trying to explain in terms of long term time horizons, compounding returns, etc. It's not worth bothering with. Like trying to talk to a dog.

62294877
There's a reason poor "people" are poor
The final redpill and the toughest to swallow is that jews are 100% right about the goyim

62294881
>>62294870
You probably come across as obnoxious and irritating and you're clearly in your early 20s and lack the maturity to keep shit to yourself. People don't just randomly get aggressive the way you've described, you're missing the key details and you're probably rubbing people up the wrong way and saying all the wrong this because you lack tact and grace.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62295086
I don’t interact with poor people much but my experience is that they get upset if they hear that they actually have agency. that there is a way out through their own actions feels like an attack on them. easier to think you’re just a victim of circumstance. so if you say I’ve done x, y, or z, they think you’re saying you’re better than them. and in a way you are saying that and you are in fact better than them. the poor are superstitious in a way that reduces the role of their own agency. if you do well, you are merely lucky. if you do badly, it has to do with fate, not your own actions.

62295139
I side with OP, poor people are irrational.

If you're 100% honest with poor working class people they will hate you.
You have to lie and say you have a job, a mortgage, problems, etc. because they don't want to hear about you winning.

Just get your money and live your life and be careful how you present yourself around poors they're like a different caste of lower human.

62295155
It's the inability to think further ahead than just the next month or year.
My Father has been showing me newspaper clippings of "GOLD PRICES SOAR HIGHER THAN EVER!" for at least the last 15 years, with the usual comment of "I should have bought Gold years ago!". We have this conversation every year, and I tell him that NOW is the time to buy, but what does he say? "No, it's so high right now, I'm sure it can't go any higher. You can only lose if you buy now!"

62295158
>>62294980
>seething this hard
You lost and lost hard, you eternal child. Go cry someplace that cares.

62295173
>>62295155
I recently saw a YouTube video where a guy showed what it would look like if you invested $1,000 every month into the S&P500 if you bought at the absolute worst time (highest price) for each month for seven years. It still made a ton of money. Even if you had the worst luck in human history and chose the worst day to invest every single time you still come out ahead.

62295177
>>62294881

>Bro i know you barely make ends meet already, but if you put 90% of your income into #FARTPEPE you'll be rich bro! I made a killing on it using mommy's creditcard that one time. Why are you so angry bro, don't you want to be a smart investor like me? Why do you hate investing? Why are you subhuman worker trash incapable of understanding simple financial advice? I'm just trying to help!

62295192
>>62295155
And when the price is down, like in 2015 where gold dropped down to $1,000 after a peak around $1,700, it’s "I’m not buying this shit lol". Whatever happens, there’s always a good excuse to do nothing.

62295196
>>62295173
>worst luck in human history
>in the longest and biggest bull market ever

62295198
>>62295173
Yeah, it's the simple saying of "Time in the market beats timing the market".
I always knew I'd be investing from the time I actually earned enough money (which wasn't until I turned 29) until I either get enough for a nice House or I turn 45. Neither has happened yet, but I sleep like a baby while watching the money grow.

62295203
>>62294870
because normalfags don't care about the mathematical trick of turning 10k into a million in 60 years

62295207
>>62294889
normalfags only talk about pussy and cock
you never touch grass

62295211
>>62295198
>Time in the market beats timing the market
meaningless bullshit unless you keep it there at least 20 years
even a 10 year period isn't enough for your meme to protect you against crashes like 2000 and 2008

62295212
>>62294870
i dont talk to people about investing. everyone knows about the stock market. some choose to participate... some choose not to.

they thought about it and dont want to invest in stocks.

62295213
>>62294980
You BTFO'd that samefagging tranny. Good job, anon. Bravo.

62295216
>>62294870
you'll understand when the stock market has a real crash, there's a reason there's lots of laws to protect pleb investors. How does losing 80% of your money and then crabbing for 30 years sound?

62295221
>>62294870
>>62294872

For a lot of people it's a mindset that many never actually obtain. Many people have a high time preference thay revolves around instant gratification. They get their monthly pay and spaff it on frivolities and consumables like drugs, alcohol or funko pop tier BS. People with low time preference understand the need for investments and savings knowing that they will benefit down the line from dividends or having a back up fund when the income dries up.
Some people learn the hard way but many simply don't learn, particularly those in welfare states.

62295226
>>62295173
>DCA for 7 years
And wait for decades to outlast any crash that might occur which basically means you're sacrificing your youth to become a wealthy geriatric. Good idea if you want to afford those Gucci diapers which are very comfy for old scrotums.

62295261
>>62295196
Unironically yes. Good luck would have meant doing the exact same thing from 2000-2012.

62295297
>>62294870
Because their news feed is filled with doomer bears who want them to fail

62295303
>>62294870
>What the hell is this psychology?
Risk aversion, which gets worse the poorer you are.

62295361
>>62294877

62295385
When the average normie has an unexpected windfall of money, the first thing that pops into their head is "what should I spend this money on?". It's like a reflex that has been embedded into them since childhood. They've been programmed by an endless stream of marketing campaigns at every stage of their life to become the ultimate consumers.

Many people are eventually able to break out of this mindset and start to invest long term, but it's usually a process that lasts several years. One or two conversations is not enough to undo a lifetime of programming

62295393
>>62295361
*takes and puts on*

62295428
>>62295385
I remember talking to an attorney who’s firm would advise a lot of lottery winners. He said it took on average 3-5 years for the lottery winners to be able to change their mindset from "spend money as fast as I can" to "I need to protect my money". Which is why like 70% of all lottery winners declare bankruptcy within 5 years. They’re not able to change their mindset fast enough and will instinctively try to spend it all

62295437
>>62295211
>bro, you need to hold AT LEAST for 40 years!
Bitchass mentality right there.
Do you even invest, or are you just trading?

62295472
>>62295428
Also every single person they've ever met will try to talk them into giving them some. They'll give in to their parents or kids or favorite cousin then the race is on
>but you gave some to X why aren't you sharing with meeeee why are you so meeeeean
And your parents/kids will say
>don't bogart that stash you should share your good fortune with X, Y, and Z and all their relatives as well
Which is why you should never ever let anybody know you've come into a windfall. Just move away and drop contact before enjoying your winnings.

62295483
>>62294877
Jews are somehow always right and somehow always win. Maybe they really are god's chosen, now I'm kind of bullish on them. How do I invest in Jews winning so I make bank?

62295630
>>62295483
Bot post

62295773
>>62294870
>Why do workers/poor hate investing?
are you retarded? most workers are poor, and they can't invest at all, their income is too low. plus, why would you invest $100 or w/e, if you can barely survive?

I'm not sure if people in this board are too shielded from reality or just deeply retarded

62295811
>>62294870
Poor and blue collar folks usually aren’t too good at long term thinking. I get the same thing with them. Whenever you talk about investing they’re like “what’s the point of money if you don’t spend it”, etc. I have a net worth of $1.5 mil at 32 from investing my money and they don’t get it.

item.title: Why hasn't cryptocurrency technology been adopted yet? — 12 replies — 48.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why hasn't cryptocurrency technology been adopted yet?

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-05-31 18:44 UTC

OP

Why hasn't cryptocurrency technology been adopted yet? Allow me to explain.
Cryptocurrencies support decentralized networks, which are used for data computation and storage. These networks are cheaper to use than traditional server infrastructure.
If it's cheaper, why don't corporations use them? One, there are regulations preventing adoption, but CLARITY will change this in the US. Two, corporations have invested trillions into building and maintaining server infrastructure. Liquidation is risky from there point of view because they don't understand decentralized networks. A few brave corporations will lead this great transition, and when they are successful, everyone will follow.
You are in the final window of being early. Don't fuck this up.

How is it that everyone on reddit are millionaires

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-05-31 18:27 UTC

OP

>excluding crypto
Seriously, do you all have more than 200k in your brokerage accounts? Its crazy to me that you Americans are that well off. Even on ticktok live streams, you see this guy presenting his 200k+ portfolio in his mid to late 20s. Others on reddit are showcasing their portfolio at mid 30 already reaching 1M+. Yet people all around me are claiming that life has gotten increasingly harder and more expensive. Here is Switzerland we are getting drained with increasing Krankenkasse (health care) costs and mandatory radio-service billings (Serafe) to the point that we are voting to decrease these this month, somehow.
This cant all be fake personal marketing and fake boasting, can it?
Statistics show that the median savings in top wealth western nations is like 50-80k (checking, savings and investments, excluding house, cars etc.) in total.
ALSO...WHERE IS ALL THIS MONEY COMING FROM TO INVEST IN THE STOCK MARKET? How are these companies being pumped so heavily with cash that giga-corps like Micron reach 1 trillion easily overnight?

>>62295508
It's called consolidation. There's very few websites of any note and only a few kooks in the developed world are consciously not online so if you're rich you're gonna be in one or two communities.

??? it's a bull market

62295633

people with high balances are more willing to share them. so there is selection bias. that said, of course many people in the US will have a higher balance. even though there are people in a ton of debt there is also an investing culture that doesn’t exist as much in Europe. my main point of reference is Germany which has a strong saving culture but not an investing one.

>buy something semi safe thats still obviously going to 2x in a year at minmum (DRAM, AMD, INTC)

>run it back with a new credit card
>free 0% borrowing against my capital, 0 margin
I know this is really risky, but in the worst case scenario theres like a 33% drop or something that doesnt recover by month 12. then I just liquidate at a loss, use that plus my own capital to repay, which would only be around 10k max. if the market drops more than 33% were all fucked anyway
the one thing you cannot allow to happen is passing the 12 months tho, because then the APR is like 25%

62286347
>>62285878
>outputs start to get a little blurry 4-5 weeks out and then just turn into noise, which is why I do the updates every 4 weeks
Why not update weekly? or even daily?

62286447
>>62286347
>Why not update weekly? or even daily?
Basically, because it's a lot easier - there are a lot of inputs into the model that each have to be harvested and processed by me before they get fed into the sim. It runs into the hours each time. I can also cope that the current date's "conditions" are obviously the starting point of the forecast, so having them flattening out a little bit is easier to align
I do weekly updates on the site and the twitter account, but that's 90% just applying current price momentum and FX momentum to the existing physics-based forecast, which I have to get better at. I'm a nerd, not a trader

62294961
>>62294724
gb2 reddit normalfag

62294979
>>62294947

Man, this argument falls apart the second you stop treating women like a hive mind.

You're basically taking a few traits you've noticed in some people and stretching them across billions of individuals. That's not insight; that's stereotyping with extra steps. There are tons of women who spend their free time reading, gaming, drawing, writing, coding, collecting random niche interests, or disappearing into obscure rabbit holes for hours. The idea that women avoid introverted hobbies "like AIDS" is just obviously false to anyone who's spent five minutes online.

The money and psychopath thing isn't much stronger. If women were only attracted to wealth, status, and flashy antisocial behavior, then shy guys, nerds, artists, academics, and regular dudes would never date. Yet they do, every day. Reality keeps refusing to cooperate with the theory.

And the "normalfags are female-minded" line is weird too. Most people care about relationships because relationships are a major part of human life. That's true for men and women. Having goals outside of niche hobbies doesn't mean someone worships sex. It just means they have different priorities.

The claim that women prefer low IQ is probably the funniest part. In the real world, competence, intelligence, and capability are generally attractive traits. Not universally, because humans are complicated, but far more often than the caricature you're describing.

The biggest problem with this worldview is that it tries to reduce millions of different people into one cynical template. Some women are introverts. Some are extroverts. Some care about money a lot. Some barely care at all. Some like athletes. Some like engineers. Some like artists. Human beings are way too varied to fit into a single edgy slogan.

At some point the theory starts looking less like an observation about women and more like a story you're telling yourself about them.
